methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Amogy is a Series C climate and energy company founded by Seonghoon Woo, currently valued at $700M post-money with $300M+ raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of nine same-sector peers at Series B through Series D stages, comprising eight private companies and one public listing (LNZA). Key private comparables include KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D), and Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $1.2B from the current $700M base. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company Climate & Energy comparables yields $500M, with peer valuations spanning $120M to $6.0B. The median peer valuation of $500M reflects the full range including both lower-capitalized and significantly larger frontier-tech companies in the sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M to $1.4B, with a midpoint of $845M. The current $700M post-money valuation sits within this range but skews toward the lower end, approximately 17% below the midpoint. Key uncertainties include stage-specific multiple assumptions and the heterogeneity within the peer set—the span from $120M to $6.0B indicates substantial valuation variance across Climate & Energy stage-adjacent companies, reflecting different technology maturity and capital intensity profiles.
